rationale: Both catch the latent gap (diffusion-side hunyuan_image3_transformer.py still calling the removed get_cache_scale API), but X undermines itself with a fabricated 'major' claim that HunyuanModel.load_weights isn't covered by an outer AutoWeightsLoader — directly contradicted by the ground-truth reviewer's own statement that this is a 'delegated loader' relying on the outer AutoWeightsLoader, and by Y's verified citation of the actual import/upstream mapping code. Y independently validates the design against real upstream vLLM source (matching lishunyang12's conclusions almost exactly) and its non-blocking note about the test bypassing AutoWeightsLoader closely mirrors the ground-truth reviewer's concern about the test not proving real-checkpoint resolution. X's redundant triple-repeated review_text also suggests a formatting/reliability issue, though this wasn't scored directly.
